title: Nobel Prize in Physics 1921
content: Albert Einstein was awarded the 1921 Nobel Prize in Physics for his explanation of the photoelectric effect, not for his theory of relativity. The prize was announced in 1922 and presented to him in 1923.

title: Discovery of Penicillin
content: Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin in 1928 at St Mary's Hospital in London. He noticed that a mold (Penicillium notatum) inhibited bacterial growth on a petri dish. Howard Florey and Ernst Boris Chain later developed it into a medicine.

title: Speed of Light
content: The speed of light in vacuum is exactly 299,792,458 meters per second (approximately 3 × 10^8 m/s). This is a defined constant in the International System of Units since 1983.

title: Human Genome Project
content: The Human Genome Project was completed in April 2003, having begun in 1990. It determined the sequence of approximately 3 billion base pairs that make up human DNA. The project was an international collaboration led by the US National Institutes of Health.

title: Calculus Discovery
content: Both Isaac Newton and Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz independently developed calculus in the late 17th century. Newton developed his 'method of fluxions' around 1665-1666 but did not publish until later. Leibniz published his work in 1684. The priority dispute between them was one of the most famous controversies in the history of mathematics.

title: Structure of DNA
content: James Watson and Francis Crick published the double helix structure of DNA in 1953, based significantly on X-ray crystallography data produced by Rosalind Franklin and Maurice Wilkins at King's College London. Photo 51, taken by Franklin, was crucial to the discovery.

title: Theory of General Relativity
content: Albert Einstein published his theory of general relativity in 1915. It describes gravity as the curvature of spacetime caused by mass and energy. The theory was confirmed by Arthur Eddington's observation of light bending during the 1919 solar eclipse.

title: CRISPR-Cas9
content: CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing was developed by Jennifer Doudna and Emmanuelle Charpentier, who published their key paper in 2012. They received the 2020 Nobel Prize in Chemistry. CRISPR stands for Clustered Regularly Interspaced Short Palindromic Repeats.

title: Moon Landing
content: Apollo 11 landed on the Moon on July 20, 1969. Neil Armstrong was the first person to walk on the lunar surface, followed by Buzz Aldrin. Michael Collins remained in lunar orbit. Armstrong's famous words were 'That's one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind.'

title: Periodic Table
content: Dmitri Mendeleev published his periodic table in 1869, organizing elements by atomic weight and predicting the existence of undiscovered elements including gallium, scandium, and germanium. Lothar Meyer independently developed a similar table around the same time.
